A.text: Each hexagon is adjacent only to pentagons.

B.text: Two pentagons share a common edge.

C.text: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.

D.text: No two hexagons share a common edge.

parsed.answer_value: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.
parsed.rationale: The image shows a spherical molecular structure composed of interconnected polygons, characteristic of a fullerene. In such structures, the fundamental rule is that every pentagon must be surrounded by hexagons to maintain the closed spherical shape without adjacent pentagons. Observing the connections in the diagram confirms that no two pentagons share an edge, and each pentagon is bordered exclusively by hexagonal faces.
